title: BugRecord
description: A single captured bug, persisted in the consumer repo's tracked ledger at .straitjacket/bugs.json (wrapper shape {"bugs": [BugRecord, ...]}). Written by the report-bug skill the moment a bug is found, BEFORE any remote mirroring, so a bug is never lost if a backend is absent or the host workflow is interrupted. The ledger is meant to be committed. Three fields form the bridge to the straitjacket coverage-reviewer so a later regression/tdd run can lift a bug into a test work unit without prose-parsing: suspect_files -> WorkUnit.target_file, suspect_symbol -> WorkUnit.target_symbol, intended_behavior_seed -> WorkUnit.intended_behavior.

id.description: Stable, human-readable, collision-resistant identifier. Format: bug-YYYY-MM-DD-NN where NN is a zero-padded per-day counter (next unused index among existing same-day ids in the ledger). NOT a UUID — the skill has no randomness source and a date+counter is sortable and readable.

title.description: One-line summary, pattern '[Area] symptom'. Reused verbatim as the GitHub issue title / Jira summary.

created.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) the bug was captured. Use the current date from the session environment.

status.description: Lifecycle. 'open' = captured locally only. 'mirrored' = a remote issue/ticket exists (see remote). 'fixed'/'wontfix'/'duplicate' set later when the bug is resolved or merged into another record.

severity.description: Impact. Drives GitHub label and Jira priority when mirrored.

summary.description: 1-3 sentence description of the problem in prose.

steps_to_reproduce.description: Ordered reproduction steps. May be empty if not yet known; capture what is known rather than blocking.

expected.description: Observable behavior that SHOULD occur (return value, error type, side effect, UI state). Half of the test oracle.

actual.description: Observable behavior that DOES occur (the defect). The other half of the test oracle.

suspect_files.description: Repo-relative path(s) believed to contain the defect. CONSUMPTION BRIDGE: maps to WorkUnit.target_file when a tdd target/fix-mode run lifts this bug into a test work unit. Best-effort; may be empty when unknown.

suspect_symbol.description: Function/method/type believed responsible, language-qualified (Rust: Parser::parse_header; C#: Parser.ParseHeader). CONSUMPTION BRIDGE: maps to WorkUnit.target_symbol. Optional.

intended_behavior_seed.description: A contract-style sentence describing what the correct behavior is, phrased so a coverage-reviewer can lift it into a write-once WorkUnit.intended_behavior anchor. Derive from expected-vs-actual, framed as the rule the fixed code must satisfy (e.g. 'parse_header rejects a header longer than 4 KiB with HeaderTooLong instead of truncating'). CONSUMPTION BRIDGE: this is the field that makes the ledger test-context-ready.

error_signature.description: Error type / message / trimmed stack excerpt, if any. Key phrases, not a full stack dump. Used for local dedupe matching and remote issue body.

environment.description: Platform / version / OS / config relevant to reproduction. Optional.

discovered_during.description: Provenance — how the bug surfaced. Free text, e.g. 'manual', 'straitjacket:tdd run <run_id>', 'tdd stage D', 'code review'. Records the 'stay on task' context the skill exists to preserve.

labels.description: Freeform tags applied locally and propagated to GitHub labels / Jira labels when mirrored.

remote.description: Populated AFTER successful mirroring. Absent or empty while status is 'open'.

repo.description: owner/name the issue was created in.

related.description: Other bug ids in this ledger, or remote keys/URLs, this record relates to (regression-of, duplicate-of, blocks).

notes.description: Freeform follow-up notes appended over the bug's life.
